parsed.summary: The cumulative v0.4.0→v0.4.6 sweep (84 files, 6600+ insertions) cannot be adequately reviewed from the provided summary alone. The diff stat confirms scripts/lib/process.mjs was entirely deleted, and multiple core modules lack corresponding test files. The commit log shows six separate hotfix rounds, which correlates with higher residual-defect probability. I was unable to inspect the actual diff content—this review is therefore incomplete and the findings below reflect only structural/observational risks.

findings.title: Deleted process.mjs may leave broken import chains
findings.body: scripts/lib/process.mjs shows 67 lines of pure deletion (no additions). If any remaining file still contains `import ... from './process.mjs'` or `from './lib/process.mjs'`, it will throw ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND at runtime. This class of bug is invisible in diff-stat review and survives until the affected code path is exercised. The neighbouring files (render.mjs, retry.mjs, glm-client.mjs) were all modified in this sweep, making stale-import survival plausible.
findings.recommendation: Grep the entire codebase for any remaining reference to 'process.mjs' (or its exported symbols). If found, either update the import target or confirm the re-export was intentional. Add a CI check that validates all static imports resolve (e.g., `node --check` or import-tracing tool).

findings.title: tracked-jobs.mjs has no dedicated test coverage
findings.body: scripts/lib/tracked-jobs.mjs (78 lines changed) manages job-tracking state—a domain prone to race conditions, stale-state bugs, and partial-failure corruption. No corresponding test file (e.g., tests/tracked-jobs.test.mjs) appears in the changed-files list or tests/ directory. The tests/ directory contains coverage for retry, render, state, args, fs, bigmodel-errors, and others, but not tracked-jobs. This is a blind spot: job tracking failures are expensive to detect in production and hard to recover from.
findings.recommendation: Add a tests/tracked-jobs.test.mjs covering at minimum: (1) normal add/query/remove lifecycle, (2) concurrent job registration, (3) persistence across process restart, (4) cleanup of stale/orphaned job entries. Block merge until coverage exists.

findings.title: glm-client.mjs core module lacks direct test coverage
findings.body: scripts/lib/glm-client.mjs (155 lines changed) is the primary API client handling authentication, request dispatch, error mapping, and retry delegation. The test files present cover retry.test.mjs (retry policy), bigmodel-errors.test.mjs (error classification), and result-propagation.test.mjs, but none appears to directly exercise glm-client.mjs's own orchestration logic—particularly the interaction between BigModel error dispatch, retry, and job tracking. A regression in the client's error-to-retry wiring would silently drop requests or cause infinite retry loops.
findings.recommendation: Add integration-level tests for glm-client.mjs that exercise the full error→classify→retry→succeed/fail path with mocked HTTP responses. Verify that BigModel error codes map to the correct retry strategies and that exhausted retries surface meaningful errors rather than silent hangs.

next_steps: Run `git diff v0.4.0..HEAD` and inspect every file for remaining imports of the deleted scripts/lib/process.mjs.
Run `grep -r 'process\.mjs' scripts/ commands/` to confirm no stale references exist.
Add tests/tracked-jobs.test.mjs before shipping; job-tracking state corruption is high-impact and hard to detect in production.
Add at least one integration test for glm-client.mjs covering the retry-backoff-error-classification pipeline.
Verify the full test suite passes against the cumulative diff: `node --test tests/*.test.mjs`.
